title: Different Gifts

pages.text: A bird sat on the branch of a huge tree and ate a delicious fruit.
The bird asked the tree, "You stand here all day long; don't you ever go out to look for food?"
The tree replied, "No, I make my own food using sunlight, air, water, and the nutrients my roots absorb from the soil."
The bird sighed sadly and said, "How lucky you are! I have to fly here and there just to find something to eat."
The tree smiled gently and said, "God has created us with different gifts, and we must be happy with what we have."

"I cannot move to see the beauty of the world, though I enjoy the wind, sky, rain, and the visitors who come to taste my fruits. But you can fly wherever you wish, so we both have things to be happy about."
Hearing this, the bird finally realized its own wonderful potential.
It felt a little ashamed for not being grateful for its freedom and wings.
"I take your fruit and even build my nest in your branches, but I don't give you anything in return," the bird said sadly.
The tree smiled again and replied, "But you do! You spread my seeds far and wide so new trees can grow, and one day, your body will return to the earth to feed the soil. We help each other."
The bird happily flew away, thinking, "God has given every living being different capabilities, and we should always be content with who we are."

moral: Everyone has unique gifts and abilities. Be grateful for your own strengths, appreciate others, and use your talents to help the world.
